pattern: A search succeeds only when a stored word has the same length and differs in exactly one position, so grouping words by length and counting mismatches answers each query directly.
hints: The replaced word must keep the same length, so only stored words of equal length can ever match.
Exactly one character must change — zero differences (the same word) does not count, and two or more differences is too many.
Bucket the dictionary by word length. For a query, scan only the words of its length and return true the moment one differs in exactly one position.

edge_inputs.description: the only true case changes exactly one character to reach a stored word
an identical word is false because zero characters change
two differing positions is false; a different length never matches
single-character words: any other single character matches

stress.description: many words over a small alphabet then queries that are near-misses, exact, or unrelated
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abc'
    n = min(max(1, size), 90)
    words = set()
    while len(words) < n:
        L = rng.randint(1, 5)
        words.add(''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(L)))
    words = list(words)
    ops = ['MagicDictionary', 'buildDict']
    args = [[], [words]]
    for _ in range(min(90, n)):
        base = rng.choice(words)
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.4 and len(base) > 0:
            i = rng.randrange(len(base))
            repl = rng.choice(letters)
            q = base[:i] + repl + base[i+1:]
        elif r < 0.7:
            q = base
        else:
            L = rng.randint(1, 6)
            q = ''.join(rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(L))
        ops.append('search'); args.append([q])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: small alphabet and short words force many one-off matches and exact-word traps
